title: Mildly Charred Syrian Lamb Kebabs
description: Snappy and cozy Syrian dish featuring grape leaves and char-grilled lamb, perfect for a weeknight dinner. Dairy-free friendly and served in scallop shell vessels.

ingredients.name: Grape leaves, fresh or preserved in brine
Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Mince or press for best flavor

ingredients.name: Sumac
ingredients.notes: Substitute with paprika or oregano if unavailable

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Choose a mild or medium strength oil

instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 425°F (220°C).
In a bowl, whisk together sumac, lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and garlic. Add sliced lamb and mix until well coated.
Thread lamb onto skewers, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each piece.
Place a grape leaf on each skewer, tucking the edges under the lamb. This will prevent juices from escaping during cooking.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the lamb reaches 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare.
Transfer the kebabs to a plate and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve each kebab in a scallop shell vessel, garnished with additional grape leaves if desired.
Repeat the cooking process with remaining lamb and grape leaves.
